goal: Unify gad.exe + gad-tui.exe into a SINGLE binary with argv-based mode dispatch. Today the install dir at %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\gad\bin\ holds TWO separately-compiled bun binaries at ~115MB each (~230MB total, down to ~115MB after 2026-04-22 dupe deletion). Industry convention (claude, codex, gemini, opencode, free-code — all bun+TSX+React+Ink+single-binary-argv-dispatch) is ONE binary where 'gad' (no args) enters interactive/TUI mode and 'gad <subcommand>' runs non-interactive. PROVEN pattern — tmp/free-code/package.json bin 'claude' -> ./cli, entrypoint src/entrypoints/cli.tsx, bun build --compile via scripts/build.ts. Same tech stack as GAD. No spike needed. Action: (1) move packages/gad-tui/src/entry.tsx export to a default async function runTui(argv) that boots the Ink app, keep as workspace package for dev UX, (2) rewrite bin/commands/tui.cjs to require('@magicborn/gad-tui') + invoke runTui(rawArgs) — no spawnSync, no sibling .exe, (3) bin/gad.cjs: if no subcommand, route to 'tui' subcommand — matches claude/codex convention where bare invocation = interactive, (4) scripts/build-bun-release.mjs drops the separate pnpm --filter @magicborn/gad-tui build:bun call + drops the TUI artifact copy step; single bun build --compile --target=bun-<platform> bin/gad.cjs produces the complete binary, (5) install script produces ONE gad.exe (per 63-49), (6) bin/commands/tui.cjs 'gad-tui is not installed' error path deleted — impossible state, (7) legacy backward-compat: if require('@magicborn/gad-tui') throws, check for sibling gad-tui.exe and spawn (current behavior) for N releases, then drop. Effect: ~115MB saved per install, eliminates 2-artifact install problem, matches industry convention, supersedes 63-48 (TUI-in-CI becomes unnecessary once there's no separate TUI artifact).
